title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants two fixes: (1) In the test script, tests 2/3/4/5/7 should capture the exit code with `|| exit_code=$?` and assert it, matching the pattern already used in tests 1 and 6, so failures produce diagnostics instead of silent aborts under `set -euo pipefail`. (2) In the container images doc, replace the `which gcc; echo $?` verification command with a pattern that actually propagates a non-zero exit code when gcc is absent, since `echo $?` always makes the overall command exit 0.

code_comments.body:
Tests 2, 3, 4, 5, and 7 invoke the script-under-test without `|| exit_code=$?`:

```bash
(cd "$workdir" && bash "$SCRIPT_UNDER_TEST" "2025.2")
```

Under `set -euo pipefail`, if the script exits non-zero (e.g. because `sed -i` isn't available, or the constraints file can't be modified), the test script aborts immediately — before any `assert_file_contains` / `assert_file_not_contains` calls run, and before the `=== Results ===` summary is ever printed. The caller only sees an abrupt non-zero exit with no diagnostic output.

This is the same pattern fixed in Tests 1 and 6 of this file. For consistency, every invocation that is expected to succeed should still capture and assert the exit code:

```bash
# Example fix for Test 2
local exit_code=0
(cd "$workdir" && bash "$SCRIPT_UNDER_TEST" "2025.2") || exit_code=$?
assert_eq "script exits 0" "0" "$exit_code"
```

Apply the same idiom at lines 77, 101, 126, and 163.

`````markdown
```

Under `set -euo pipefail`, if the script exits non-zero (e.g. because `sed -i` isn't available, or the constraints file can't be modified), the test script aborts immediately — before any `assert_file_contains` / `assert_file_not_contains` calls run, and before the `=== Results ===` summary is ever printed. The caller only sees an abrupt non-zero exit with no diagnostic output.

This is the same pattern fixed in Tests 1 and 6 of this file. For consistency, every invocation that is expected to succeed should still capture and assert the exit code:

```bash
# Example fix for Test 2
local exit_code=0
(cd "$workdir" && bash "$SCRIPT_UNDER_TEST" "2025.2") || exit_code=$?

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````

</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: All five test functions (Tests 2, 3, 4, 5, and 7) now capture the exit code with `local exit_code=0` before invocation, use `|| exit_code=$?` on the script call, and assert it with `assert_eq "exit code is 0" "0" "$exit_code"`. This matches the existing pattern in Tests 1 and 6 and prevents silent aborts under `set -euo pipefail`.

code_comments.body:
The verification command always exits 0 — unsuitable for scripted checks:

```bash
docker run --rm c5c3/keystone:28.0.0 sh -c 'which gcc; echo $?'
# Expected output: 1 (gcc not found)
```

This always exits 0. `echo $?` captures and prints the exit code of `which gcc` as text, but since `echo` itself exits 0, `sh -c` exits 0, and `docker run` exits 0. A developer who wraps this in an `if` statement or CI script will always see success — even if gcc IS present in the image.

A more reliable pattern:

```bash
# Verify no build tools in final image
docker run --rm c5c3/keystone:28.0.0 which gcc \
  && echo "FAIL: gcc found in image" \
  || echo "PASS: gcc not found"
```

Or for a script that propagates failure:

```bash
! docker run --rm c5c3/keystone:28.0.0 which gcc
```

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
```

This always exits 0. `echo $?` captures and prints the exit code of `which gcc` as text, but since `echo` itself exits 0, `sh -c` exits 0, and `docker run` exits 0. A developer who wraps this in an `if` statement or CI script will always see success — even if gcc IS present in the image.

A more reliable pattern:

```bash
# Verify no build tools in final image
docker run --rm c5c3/keystone:28.0.0 which gcc \
  && echo "FAIL: gcc found in image" \
  || echo "PASS: gcc not found"
```

Or for a script that propagates failure:

```bash
! docker run --rm c5c3/keystone:28.0.0 which gcc
```

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: The verification command was replaced from `docker run --rm c5c3/keystone:28.0.0 sh -c 'which gcc; echo $?'` to `docker run --rm c5c3/keystone:28.0.0 which gcc && echo "FAIL: gcc found in image" || echo "PASS: gcc not found"`, which correctly propagates the exit code and provides clear pass/fail output suitable for scripted checks.

resolution_summary: Both review comments were fully addressed. In tests/scripts/test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh, Tests 2, 3, 4, 5, and 7 were updated to use the `local exit_code=0; ... || exit_code=$?; assert_eq` idiom, matching the pattern already present in Tests 1 and 6. In docs/reference/container-images.md, the build-tool verification command was changed from `sh -c 'which gcc; echo $?'` (which always exits 0) to `which gcc && echo 'FAIL' || echo 'PASS'` (which correctly propagates the exit code). No changes were made to files or locations beyond those mentioned in the review.
